level_signals: 2–5+ years in revenue operations, billing operations, finance operations, business operations, deal desk, or SaaS operations
Title is Senior Analyst, not Manager
Reports to / works closely with a Quote-to-Cash lead
JD mentions growth path 'into broader RevOps, Q2C, or systems roles over time'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 2–5+ years in revenue operations, billing operations, finance operations, business operations, deal desk, or SaaS operations
Exceptional attention to detail and operational rigor
Familiarity with Salesforce or similar business systems
Ability to manage multiple workflows in a fast-paced environment
Strong communication and cross-functional collaboration skills

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience supporting SaaS quote-to-cash or billing workflows
Experience working with CPQ, billing, or subscription systems
Experience in high-growth B2B SaaS or AI companies
Familiarity with subscription billing and recurring revenue models

scope.surface: Quote-to-cash operations, billing, renewals, invoicing, and commercial infrastructure
primary_metrics_inferred: Accuracy and scalability of quote-to-cash operations
Billing reconciliation and invoice QA quality
Data quality and process consistency across systems
Operational efficiency across billing and commercial workflows
core_responsibilities: Support day-to-day quote-to-cash workflows across quoting, billing, invoicing, renewals, and amendments
Help ensure accuracy and operational integrity across commercial processes
Review and validate order, subscription, and billing data
Assist with invoice QA and billing reconciliation
Partner closely with Finance on operational billing processes and reporting
Help identify operational inefficiencies and areas for automation
Work closely with the Quote-to-Cash lead on process improvements and workflow optimization
Support operational workflows across Salesforce, Nue, billing systems, and related tooling
Help maintain data quality and process consistency across systems

green_flags: Company has reached $100M+ ARR — strong product-market fit signal
Clear reporting structure with a Quote-to-Cash lead
Explicit growth path mentioned ('broader RevOps, Q2C, or systems roles over time')
Comprehensive benefits package disclosed in detail (medical, dental, vision, 401K match, unlimited PTO)
Company-provided daily lunch and in-person environment
Real customer logos disclosed (Cleary Gottlieb, Linklaters, Barclays, etc.)
red_flags: 5 days per week onsite requirement in NYC Union Square — no remote or hybrid flexibility
Years range '2–5+' is ambiguous — unclear whether 2 years is truly sufficient or whether 5+ is preferred

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior Analyst', years range, and reporting relationship to Q2C lead)
scope.business_model (inferred from '$100M+ ARR' and SaaS context in JD)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated as KPIs)
compensation.equity_mentioned (no equity mentioned anywhere in JD)
nice_to_haves id cpq_billing_subscription_systems: Nue named because it appears in the responsibilities section as a specific tool, though the nice-to-have text does not name it explicitly
ambiguities: Years range '2–5+' is ambiguous — the '+' after 5 suggests 5+ is acceptable but the range starts at 2; it is unclear if 2 years is the true floor or if this is aspirational
Visa sponsorship is not addressed anywhere in the JD; marked as 'unclear'
